rows.description: Computus Joannis Longbeare Rectoris a die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutum 1731
rows.notes: Top title is partly faded and some letters (surname and a few Latin words) are abbreviated; I reconstructed the Latin phrase from context and common ledger phrasing ('Computus ... a die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutum 1731'). Characters are legible but not crystal-clear.
rows.section_header: Computus Joannis Longbeare Rectoris a die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutum 1731

rows.notes: Centered section heading clearly written. Letters are legible and there are no amounts on this row.
Amounts (10 0 0) are very clear and aligned to the three ruling columns; description short but legible.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Guinear (tenant or estate).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Guinear, amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings are clear (26, 12). Pence reads as '9' with a small loop/mark after it interpreted as the halfpenny 'ob' symbol (so 9½d). The loop is faint which reduces confidence slightly.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from the Long Wittenham estate.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Long Wittenham, amounting to £26 12s 9½d.

rows.notes: Amount (40 0 0) aligns well with the pounds/shillings/pence columns and is clear. The place name is slightly ambiguous in spelling but readable enough.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Minchinset (estate or tenancy).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Minchinset, amounting to £40 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Large pound figure (145 0 0) is unambiguous and aligns exactly with the pounds column; 'South Newington Rect.' is clearly written.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from the rectory of South Newington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from South Newington rectory, amounting to £145 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount (20 0 0) clear and well aligned. The place name appears as 'Eddington' (or similar); spelling is typical variant but readable.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from the rectory of Eddington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Eddington rectory, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: All three monetary columns read clearly as 14 pounds, 8 shillings, 10 pence. Description 'Chase-hill' is legible.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from the Chase Hill property.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Chase Hill, amounting to £14 8s 10d.

rows.notes: Amount (2 0 0) is clear. Name reads as 'Lucas Eaton' with good legibility.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Lucas Eaton (tenant or payer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Lucas Eaton, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary columns read as 14, 3, 3. Description appears to be 'The Quit Rents' (handwriting slightly compressed), so reading is moderately certain.
rows.english_description: Arrears received for quit rents.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quit rents received in arrears, amounting to £14 3s 3d.

rows.notes: Amounts 0 7 6 are clearly aligned. The description is longer but readable as 'Ralph Marshals Capons'; spacing and capitals support this reading.
rows.english_description: Capons (poultry) received from Ralph Marshal as arrears in kind.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Capons received from Ralph Marshal in arrears, valued at £0 7s 6d.

rows.notes: Entry reads 10 pounds, 9 shillings, 11 pence; all three digits are clear and within valid ranges. Description 'Bigg' is short and distinct.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Bigg (tenant or payer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Bigg, amounting to £10 9s 11d.

rows.notes: Amounts 2, 19, 5 align with columns. Shillings read as 19 (close to the 20 limit but valid). The phrase 'Caleb alias Lane' is legible though the middle word is slightly cramped.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Caleb (also called Lane).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Caleb (alias Lane), amounting to £2 19s 5d.

rows.notes: Amount 0 15 0 is clear and centered in the monetary columns. 'Bannister' reads cleanly.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Bannister (tenant or payer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Bannister, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount (1 0 0) unambiguous. Description reads 'Leaden-Hall' (hyphenation and capitals visible).
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Leaden Hall (property).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Leaden Hall, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary columns clearly show 4, 12, 11. 'Sadler' is legible; small flourishes do not affect numeric reading.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Sadler (tenant or payer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Sadler, amounting to £4 12s 11d.

rows.notes: Amount (1 13 4) is clear. 'Hart Hall' reads well; spacing between words is distinct.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Hart Hall (property or tenancy).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Hart Hall, amounting to £1 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings (4,1) are clear. Pence looks like '8' with a small trailing loop interpreted as the halfpenny mark (8½d). The fractional loop is faint and slightly ambiguous, hence reduced confidence.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment received from Napper (tenant or payer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Napper, amounting to £4 1s 8½d.

rows.notes: Numeric columns read as 4, 10, 4. The place/name looks like 'Clifton Ferry' (handwritten 'Ferry' could be 'Perry' in another reading) — chosen 'Ferry' because the initial letter has a descender matching 'F'.
rows.english_description: Arrears received for the Clifton Ferry (ferry tolls or rent).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Clifton Ferry, amounting to £4 10s 4d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings (7,9) are clear. Pence appears as 11 with a small halfpenny loop after it -> interpreted as 11½d. The fractional mark is visible but not perfectly sharp.
rows.english_description: Arrears received for the brewhouse (rent or dues on the brewhouse property).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received for the brewhouse, amounting to £7 9s 11½d.

rows.notes: Amount (6 0 0) readable and well aligned. The name looks like 'Tretheven' or similar Cornish/Devon variant; spelling uncertain but entry is legible.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Tretheven (estate or tenancy).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Tretheven, amounting to £6 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary columns read 26,10,0 clearly. The description names several parties in a list; handwriting is a little compressed making exact spellings ('Sadlane'/'Sadlanee', 'Sparkes', 'Meriton') moderately uncertain.
rows.english_description: Arrears payments received from Sadlane, Sparkes and Meriton (multiple payers or tenants).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payments from Sadlane, Sparkes and Meriton, amounting to £26 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Numbers 6,13,4 are aligned and clear. Place name reads as 'Medenham' though the first vowel could be slightly ambiguous; context suggests this reading.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Medenham (estate or tenancy).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Medenham, amounting to £6 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Final visible entry shows 4 0 0 clearly. The description 'Back Gate, Boyse' (or 'Boyse') is legible; surname spelling is reasonably certain.
rows.english_description: Arrears received for Back Gate (property) and payment noted from Boyse.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received for Back Gate / from Boyse, amounting to £4 0s 0d.
